fullResponse.name: Visualizing Binary Search
fullResponse.chatResponse: I'll guide you through how Binary Search efficiently finds a target value in a sorted array by repeatedly halving the search space. We'll track the 'low', 'high', and 'mid' pointers to see the elimination process in action.

steps.title: Prerequisite: Sorted Data
steps.content: Binary Search **requires** the input array to be sorted. This allows us to make a logical assumption: if our target is greater than the middle element, it *must* be in the right half, and vice versa. We initialize two pointers: `low` at index 0 and `high` at the last index.

steps.title: The Midpoint Logic
steps.content: In each step, we calculate the middle index: `mid = floor((low + high) / 2)`. By checking this single element, we can effectively discard half of the remaining search space, leading to an efficient logarithmic time complexity of $O(\log n)$.

steps.title: Comparison & Narrowing
steps.content:
1. If `arr[mid] == target`, we've found it!
2. If `arr[mid] < target`, the target must be to the right, so we set `low = mid + 1`.
3. If `arr[mid] > target`, the target must be to the left, so we set `high = mid - 1`.

visualHistory.name: Visualizing Binary Search
